articles.title: How Clan Dynamics Shape Al-Shabaab Recruitment
articles.excerpt: A granular examination of how clan marginalization, economic grievance, and ideological framing combine to sustain Al-Shabaab's recruiting pipeline across southern Somalia.

articles.title: Ethiopia's Fragile Peace: What the Pretoria Agreement Left Unresolved
articles.excerpt: Critical appraisal of the Cessation of Hostilities Agreement, the implementation gaps in DDR, and the risk of renewed conflict in Amhara region.

articles.title: Berbera Port and the New Competition for the Horn
articles.excerpt: How DP World's investment in Berbera has transformed the port into a geopolitical flashpoint, drawing in Ethiopian demand, Emirati capital, and FGS opposition.

articles.title: Eritrea's Long Game in the Horn
articles.excerpt: Isaias Afwerki's strategic calculus, Eritrea's role in the Tigray war, and how Asmara is repositioning as a regional spoiler or potential stabilizer.

articles.title: Somali National Army: Building Capacity Under Fire
articles.excerpt: Scholarly assessment of SNA force generation since 2012, examining donor coordination failures, clan integration in officer corps, and Danab's operational record.

articles.title: The Israel-Somaliland Rapprochement: Drivers and Limits
articles.excerpt: Analysis of what motivated Somaliland President Irro's outreach to Israel — intelligence cooperation, arms, and symbolic recognition — and the constraints imposed by Gulf patron relationships.

articles.title: UAE's Strategic Footprint Across the Horn of Africa
articles.excerpt: Mapping Emirati military, commercial, and diplomatic investments from Eritrea to Somaliland to Djibouti, and how Abu Dhabi thinks about the Red Sea as a strategic perimeter.

articles.title: Food Security Collapse in the Sahel-Horn Corridor
articles.excerpt: How simultaneous climate shocks, conflict displacement, and import price surges following Ukraine combined to push parts of Somalia toward famine in 2022.

reports.title: Somalia: Al-Shabaab Five Years After Westgate
reports.excerpt: A comprehensive assessment of Al-Shabaab's evolution, financing mechanisms, and territorial control following the Westgate attack.

reports.title: Financing Al-Shabaab: The Vital Port of Kismayo
reports.excerpt: Detailed investigation into revenue streams at Kismayo port, documenting how Al-Shabaab extracts taxation from charcoal, livestock, and goods traffic.

reports.title: The Tigray War and Its Regional Implications
reports.excerpt: Analysis of the conflict's humanitarian trajectory and spillover risks for the Horn, including Eritrean involvement and fragmentation of ENDF command structures.

reports.title: Red Sea Security and the Houthi Maritime Campaign
reports.excerpt: Strategic assessment of Houthi drone and missile attacks on commercial shipping in the Red Sea, their impact on global trade, and coalition responses.

reports.title: ATMIS Transition and the Security Vacuum in Somalia
reports.excerpt: Evaluation of the ATMIS drawdown timeline and Somalia's capacity to absorb security responsibilities, identifying critical gaps in Danab integration and logistics.

reports.title: Somaliland's Strategic Position in a Contested Horn
reports.excerpt: Examination of Somaliland's diplomatic outreach, the Berbera Corridor project, and competing interests of UAE, Ethiopia, and the FGS in the Gulf of Aden.

reports.title: Djibouti's Balancing Act: Great Power Competition
reports.excerpt: How Djibouti manages Chinese, US, French, and Japanese basing arrangements simultaneously while extracting maximum economic rent from its geographic position.

reports.title: Kenya's Northern Frontier and Cross-Border Insecurity
reports.excerpt: Analysis of Al-Shabaab's cross-border operations into Kenya, clan dynamics in the NFD, and the effectiveness of Kenya's counter-insurgency strategy.

books.title: Al-Shabaab in Somalia: The History and Ideology
books.excerpt: The definitive scholarly account of Al-Shabaab's origins, internal divisions, international connections, and evolution from ICU offshoot to territorial insurgency.

books.title: The Horn of Africa: State Formation and Decay
books.excerpt: Clapham's comparative analysis of how Ethiopia, Eritrea, Somalia, and Djibouti have each navigated the tension between sovereignty and territorial control.

books.title: War in the Horn: The Crisis of Governance and Ethnicity
books.excerpt: Analysis of ethnic federalism's contradictions in Ethiopia and how TPLF-center tension produced the conditions for the 2020 Tigray conflict.

books.title: Eritrea: How the Dream Died
books.excerpt: Chronicles Eritrea's transformation from liberation movement to one of Africa's most repressive states under Isaias Afwerki's PFDJ rule.

books.title: Making Sense of the Somali Crisis
books.excerpt: An insider account of Somali state collapse and reconstruction efforts, drawing on decades of firsthand experience in Somali governance and diplomacy.

books.title: Somalia: A Country in Peril, a Policy Nightmare
books.excerpt: Menkhaus's foundational analysis of Somali statelessness, the clan system's role in resilience and fragmentation, and the limits of external intervention.
